records.specific_details: Following a report on Safe Harbour Project, based in Mosney Village it was noted that current policies and procedures in IPAS centres on reporting and responding to DSGBV have not been reviewed since 2014.  We have lobbied local TDs to raise the issue in the Dail and call for a review of the current policies and procedures relating to domestic, sexual and gender based violence in place to support staff, management, residents, and agencies in IPAS centres
records.intended_results: Updated polices and guidelines for IPAS Centres that offer more support to centres on how to recognize and respond appropriately to a situation of domestic abuse within their centre an dkeep the victims safe
lobbying_activities: Circulation of the Evaluation of the Safe Harbour Report with recommendations outlined for change in policyRequest to mainstream the Outreach & Education Role through CUAN and update in policy for IPAS centres - Email
Discussion on the recommendations in the report and ask to mainstream the role within CUAN and update in policy for IPAS Centres - Meeting

records.specific_details: Pre-Budget Communication to the Seanad, Ministers and TD's highlighting the issues facing people with intellectual disabilities and their families, and the critical sustainability issues that are facing the organisations providing supports for these individuals - this includes St Michael's House.
records.intended_results: In Budget 2025, it is essential that the Government commits to fully resourcing and implementing the Action Plan for Disability Services 2024-2026.

records.specific_details: Drogheda & District Chamber contacted the 5 local TDs seeking support in lobbying An Coimisiún Pleanála on the new IDA Park for Drogheda’s northside. The IDA applied in 2023, with Louth County Council granting approval in June 2024 subject to conditions. The decision was appealed, but deadlines of Nov 18, 2024, and Mar 21, 2025, passed without a ruling, with delays cited due to case volume. The Chamber has urged a swift decision.
records.intended_results: A decision by An Coimisiun Pleanala in relation to the new IDA Park on the northside of Drogheda.
lobbying_activities: Communication made on 15th July 2025. - Email

records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

Gave update on our findings and international best practice.

records.specific_details: Dog Control & Sheep Worrying
records.intended_results: To highlight that sheep farmers are affected by the irresponsible behaviour of some dog owners more than any other sector.::To recognise that Comhairle na Tuaithe and its stakeholders (such as Leave no Trace and others) a lot of time and resources is being put into championing good behaviour, predominantly via Educational/Media campaigns, but unfortunately more is needed.::To highlight that some of the issues are because, politically and administratively responsibilities were scattered somewhat rather than falling under the remit of one body. This should improve with the Department of Agriculture taking up the reigns, but Dog Control must be fully resourced, with measures implemented in a timely manner and more importantly enforced.::To emphasise that lack of enforcement of obligations on dog owners and low levels of sanctions in this area are allowing this behaviour continue with sheep farmers having their flocks savaged by dogs on an almost daily basis at this point.::To highlight th

221+ Patient Representative Lyn Fenton emailed the following inviting them to attend the briefing: Norma Foley minister for children 10/06/25Michael Cahill TD 10/06/25Michael Healy Rae 10/06/25 - Email
221+ Patient Representative Una Kealy invited by email Mary Butler, John Cummins and David Cullinane. - Email
221+ Patient Representative Una Kealy spoke to David Cullinane informally post briefing event on seeing him pass by about some of the issues 221+ had just presented. - Informal communication

221+ Staff invited Oireachtas members who had attended previous similar sessions/we were in contact with by email: Ciarán Ahern, William Aird, Ivana Bacik, Catherine Ardagh, Evanne Ní Chuilinn, Malcolm Byrne, Jennifer Carroll MacNeill, David CullinaneThose who attended were also sent a follow up email by 221+ staff, the following said they will raise the issues / consider our pre-budget submission: Senator Noonan, Senator Ní Chuilinn, Mr. Jack Chambers TD - Email
Update briefing in A/V Room , Leinster House, open to all Oireachtas members. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Tobacco harm reduction and future regulation of nicotine products
records.intended_results: To share information, to ask to raise issues with parliamentary colleagues and/or in the Dáil or Seanad, and/or for a meeting to discuss PJ Carroll & Co's positions on tobacco harm reduction, retail licensing and the future regulation of nicotine products in Ireland
lobbying_activities: Follow ups on email outreach to schedule a meeting - Phone call
Outreach and follow up seeking a meeting or to share information - Email
Meeting

records.specific_details: Pre-budget Submission (for 2026 Budget)
records.intended_results: Positive influence for older people on allocation and priorities of funding across a range of issues including an Independent Commissioner for Older People, tackling loneliness & isolation and the delivery of a Statutory Homecare Scheme

records.specific_details: Letter and emails seeking support for Fórsa's campaign seeking public sector pension status for school secretaries and caretakers. There was also a briefing held for Oireachtas members in the Oireachtas AV room.
records.intended_results: Seeking support for Fórsa's campaign seeking public sector pension status for school secretaries and caretakers.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Rural and Community Development

lobbying_activities: Invited by Sinn Féin Education Spokesperson, Darren O’Rourke to give a briefing in the Oireachtas AV room on the 10th of June. 35 Oireachtas members or their representatives attended the briefing. - Meeting
Many elected representatives had further engagement with Fórsa regarding this campaign, beyond the initial mass communications. - Email
